spec_match.rationale: Uses the dialog toggle event to mirror state into the URL hash — exactly the kind of integration the new event enables.

interactive.rationale: Open/close + back button + URL share — real product flow with browser history.

blurb_alignment.rationale: Card promises URL-hash-mirrored dialog state with back-button closure; page delivers.

edge_cases.rationale: Hash-based routing has known pain points (fragment changes that trigger anchor scrolling). Doesn't show coordination with multiple dialogs on the page (which one owns the hash?).

openQuestions.title: Probe anchor-scroll on hashchange
openQuestions.detail: Setting location.hash to '#dialog' can trigger anchor scrolling to an element with that ID. Add a note about how to avoid this (pushState vs hash, or naming the dialog something unguessable).

openQuestions.title: Show multi-dialog hash strategy
openQuestions.detail: On pages with multiple dialogs, who owns the URL? Add a second dialog and show how the visitor should coordinate hash ownership (last-opened wins, or a structured hash like #dialog=settings).

summary: Clever URL/Back integration enabled by the new toggle event. Could probe the anchor-scroll edge case and multi-dialog ownership.
